question: Does the tutorial cover the complete workflow requested: removing old grout, applying new epoxy sealant, and smoothing with a finishing ball?
Does the text explicitly mention the use of "two-component epoxy" (or 2-part epoxy) and the "grout finishing ball" as requested in the prompt?
Are all images presented as close-up macro shots focusing on the tile grout lines as explicitly requested?
Does the text provide actionable details for the removal process (e.g., scraping angle, force, or specific tool motion) rather than just stating "remove the grout"?
Does the text include necessary auxiliary steps such as preparation (e.g., cleaning debris/vacuuming) or post-application cleanup (e.g., wiping excess)?
Does the initial image clearly depict the "before" state with visibly black and moldy grout lines?
Are the generated images free from significant AI artifacts (e.g., distorted tools, malformed hands) and high-resolution enough to distinguish grout texture?
Does the image corresponding to the "removal" step visually show a grout removal tool engaging with the tile gaps?
Does the image corresponding to the "smoothing" step visually show a grout finishing ball (spherical tool) being used on the line?
Do the ceramic tiles (color, pattern, texture) remain visually identical across all images, ensuring the viewer understands it is the same floor section?
Is each image placed immediately adjacent to the text step it illustrates?
Is there at least one specific image provided for each major phase: removal, application, and smoothing?
Does the sequence of images exhibit a logical chronological progression of the grout state: Moldy -> Hollow/Empty -> Wet/Filled -> Smooth/Finished?
Does the image following the removal step accurately depict a "hollowed-out" channel or deep gap between the tiles, distinct from a filled line?
Does the image for the application step visually demonstrate a viscous, wet substance (the epoxy) filling the gap?
Does the final image show a smooth, clean grout line finish that clearly contrasts with the initial moldy state?
